title: Factory Session
description: Discover, inspect, and control a live you-agent-factory Factory Session, including the bounded durable JavaScript validate → start → status/result path.
openingSummary: A Factory Session is the canonical live runtime object on a running you-agent-factory host: the unit you discover, inspect, pause or resume, and address when submitting work.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: This reference covers the you-agent-factory Factory Session: how to confirm a live session exists before submit or work commands, how to inspect one session’s identity and runtime state, how to apply pause and resume where supported, and the bounded durable JavaScript path from validate through start to status and result. Use it when you need the web Factory Session surface rather than a full CLI flag dump or a packaged docs sync.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: A Factory Session is the live runtime unit on a running host—the object you discover, inspect, and control. Session list confirms something is listening before you submit work. Each live session owns its own runtime state—loaded factory, work, and relative paths—so submit and work commands must target the intended session when more than one exists. Durable JavaScript runs are still Factory Sessions; dynamic workflow is shorthand for a JavaScript orchestrator-backed session, not a separate runtime object.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Confirm a live session with session list before submitting work. Inspect one session with session show when you need its identity and runtime projection. Apply pause or resume for that same session id when you need lifecycle control, then re-read status to confirm the outcome. For durable JavaScript work, validate source without creating a session, start one durable Factory Session, then read status and result for that same session id. Open the CLI docs for the broader command matrix, submitting work for batch schema, or the Cursor dynamic workflows guide when you need MCP host setup or dynamic-workflow authoring.

sessionList.title: Discover Sessions
sessionList.body: Session list is the primary liveness check on a running host. Use it to confirm a Factory Session is accepting work before you submit or inspect. An empty list means the host responded but no live session is registered yet. A connection failure means nothing is listening on that host and port—start or attach a factory before retrying.

sessionShow.title: Inspect A Session
sessionShow.body: Session show reads one live Factory Session projection by session id, including identity and runtime fields for that session. Each live session owns its own runtime state—loaded factory, work, and relative paths. When more than one session exists, submit and work commands must target the intended session id rather than assuming the default.

lifecycle.title: Pause And Resume
lifecycle.body: Pause and resume control one live Factory Session without dropping buffered work. Pause stops automatic progression while the host can still accept inbound submissions; resume restarts processing for that same session id. Apply the control to the intended session, then confirm the outcome by re-reading session status for that same session id.

durableJavascriptSession.title: Durable JavaScript Session
durableJavascriptSession.body: Durable JavaScript runs are still Factory Sessions. Dynamic workflow is shorthand for a JavaScript orchestrator-backed session—not a separate runtime object. Use a bounded path: validate source without creating a session, start one durable Factory Session, then read status and result for that same session id. Shared inspection nouns—Dispatch, FactoryArtifact, and FactoryEvent—belong to that same Factory Session id across CLI and related surfaces.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is a web Factory Session reference for discover, inspect, lifecycle, and durable overview. It is not a full CLI flag dump, not a sync of packaged you docs, not MCP host setup, not dynamic-workflow authoring, not submitting-work batch schema, and not the OpenAPI or API reference. CLI flags, batch schema, MCP host install, and dynamic-workflow authoring stay on sibling documentation and guide pages linked from how to use and related.

links.sessionListLabel: List live sessions on the default local host:
links.sessionListEmptyNote: If the list is empty or the host is unreachable, no live session is accepting work on that host—do not submit yet.
links.sessionShowLabel: Inspect one live session by id:
links.sessionShowTargetNote: Replace <session-id> with a SESSION ID from session list. On single-session local hosts the default compatibility session is often ~default.
links.sessionPauseLabel: Pause one live session by id:
links.sessionLifecycleConfirmNote: After pause or resume, confirm the outcome with you session show <session-id> (or the matching durable status read) for that same session id before assuming the control took effect.
links.workflowValidateLabel: Validate JavaScript workflow source without creating a session:
links.workflowStartLabel: Start one durable Factory Session asynchronously:
links.workflowStatusLabel: Read lifecycle status and progress for that same session id:
links.workflowResultLabel: Read the final or partial result for that same session id:
links.durableInspectionNounsNote: Dispatch, FactoryArtifact, and FactoryEvent records for child work all point back to the same Factory Session id returned at start. Keep validate → start → status/result on one session; MCP host setup and full dynamic-workflow authoring stay on sibling pages.
